behaviors.description: Renamed files appear in their own category and are not also counted as a deletion plus an addition.
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: the failure is at the handoff between real git rename detection output and report categorization, not just isolated math. REAL-INFRA/MOCK-HIDE: rename semantics depend on real git subprocess output (for example rename status formats/similarity handling); canned fixtures can miss real edge formatting and always pass. LLM-DEP/MULTI-STEP: no AI model decisions. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is purely git diff categorization with no model output or multi-agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real temp git repo, commit a file, rename it, run the CLI against actual repo state, and assert report shows 1 rename, 0 added-from-rename, 0 deleted-from-rename.

behaviors.description: Diffs that include binary files do not cause the tool to crash; binary files are counted in the output.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: binary diff behavior is produced by real git subprocess and real file bytes. MOCK-HIDE: mocked text diff output can hide decode/pipe/crash failures that occur only with real binary blobs and real subprocess stdout/stderr. No LLM dependency, no agent chain. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text concerns binary git diffs and process robustness only.
behaviors.test_description: In a real git repo, commit then modify/add a binary file, run the CLI, assert exit code is success, no traceback, and binary file contributes to counts.

behaviors.description: Running the tool on a repository that has no commits yet produces an empty report rather than an error.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: unborn-HEAD behavior is specific to real git repository state and subprocess exit/error behavior. MOCK-HIDE: stubbing git responses can bypass real fatal messages/exit codes seen in fresh repos. No LLM or multi-step agentic dependency. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is about git repo initialization edge case only.
behaviors.test_description: Initialize a new git repo with no commits, execute CLI, assert successful run and empty structured report instead of error output.

behaviors.description: The counts in the report match the actual change history of the repository being analyzed.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is end-to-end correctness against real repository history, which requires real commits/tree/index transitions plus subprocess git queries. Integration fakes can validate internal aggregation wiring but can miss mismatches caused by actual git history semantics. No LLM dependency. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Workflow despite multi-step implementation possibility because behavior text does not require multiple agentic decisions; rejected Agentic because no model output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Construct a real repo with a known sequence of add/modify/delete/rename commits, run CLI over that history window, and assert category counts exactly equal ground truth derived from git history.

behaviors.description: The output is identical whether the changes being analyzed are staged or unstaged.
behaviors.justification: REAL-INFRA: staged vs unstaged correctness depends on real git index vs working-tree behavior and subprocess commands. MOCK-HIDE: mocked repo state often collapses index/working-tree distinctions, hiding real divergences. No AI/ML output dependence and no agent pipeline. REJECTION-GATE: rejected Agentic/Workflow because behavior text is deterministic git state equivalence only.
behaviors.test_description: In a real repo, create equivalent change sets once staged and once unstaged, run CLI in both states, and assert byte-for-byte identical structured stdout.
